About the job
About Zero to Claw
Zero to Claw is a white-glove OpenClaw setup and automation service. We deploy security-hardened, production-ready OpenClaw AI agents for businesses. Our clients range from solo operators who want a tireless digital assistant to small teams who need agents handling email triage, meeting prep, CRM workflows, and cross-app automation.
Our founders bring deep experience: Jake has led $100M+ in business transformation, and Daniel is a former Atlassian Engineer with 6+ years shipping Jira, Bitbucket, and Confluence to millions of users.

The Role
We need someone to help us deliver custom OpenClaw workflows for our clients and maintain their running instances. You'll be the person who takes a client's messy "I want my AI to do X when Y happens" request, figures out how to wire it up in OpenClaw, builds it, tests it, and makes sure it keeps running. You'll also handle troubleshooting when things break — a misconfigured MCP server, a flaky WhatsApp integration, a gateway auth issue, an agent that's burning through API credits. You'll be deploying, configuring, integrating, and debugging real systems for real clients on real deadlines.

What you'll do
Connect OpenClaw to client tools — Gmail, Notion, Slack, Jira, Salesforce, databases, and hundreds more
Build custom workflows and skills when off-the-shelf integrations don't cut it
Troubleshoot live client instances — broken integrations, runaway API costs, flaky messaging channels, agent misbehaviour
Talk to clients, understand what they need, and translate that into working automation

What will help you succeed
Comfort in a terminal — SSH, config files, logs, basic server admin
Some experience with any programming language — enough to read code, write scripts, and hit APIs
Basic understanding of networking — ports, firewalls, DNS
Familiarity with Docker
Clear communication — you'll explain technical things to non-technical people regularly
A bias toward figuring things out over waiting to be taught

Bonus points
You've played with OpenClaw, MCP, or any AI agent framework
You've deployed something to a VPS or cloud instance before
You know your way around monitoring tools (Langfuse, Grafana, etc.)
You have opinions about LLMs, tokens, and model routing
You've built a bot, a home server, a Raspberry Pi project, or anything that proves you like tinkering

You don't need to tick every box. If you're curious, resourceful, and excited about AI agents — we'd rather hear from you than not.

Why join us
You'll get production experience with AI agents that most people only read about. You'll work directly with two founders — one from Atlassian engineering, one from enterprise strategy — who'll give you real responsibility from day one, not busywork. And you'll be joining at the ground floor of a company in one of the fastest-moving spaces in tech.

To apply
Send an email to join@zerotoclaw.ai with a short note about why this interests you, your resume/LinkedIn, and ideally one example of something technical you've built or deployed (a side project, a home server, a bot, anything that shows you can ship).

About ProcurePro
Mission > To save 1 BILLION construction admin hours.
What we do > Our market leading technology helps major builders contract with their supply chain by consolidating fragmented and manual procurement processes into a single platform (currently solved by Word, Excel & email).
Recent News: Funding → by Australia’s premier venture capital fund Airtree, recently raised $6.15m in 2023: https://shorturl.at/vUpBX
2024 & 2025 LinkedIn Top Startups, https://tinyurl.com/y5eeucww

Values we live by
Better people build better companies
Whole arse it, with ruthless pragmatism
Help the customer win
(We’ll tell you more about these as discussions progress)

Our Product-Led strategy
We’re Founded by Developers and Designers (as well as domain experts) - technical excellence is in our DNA. This means:
We spend time up front properly designing new work, testing with customers etc, whilst solving fun technical problems.
We continually invest in testing/quality - we’ve written 10,000+ automated tests in the last 2 years, we're huge advocates for static analysis & type safety, and our infrastructure is automated with Terraform.
Our entire team (including our CEO) is bought into this mindset, as we're reaping the rewards of shipping quality code, avoiding re-work & moving fast as a team.
We're actively rebuilding our processes to be AI-native, not just AI-assisted, and we want engineers who wants to help shape this transition

Our Tech Stack
Backend is Symfony 6 + PHP 8
Frontend is 100% Typescript + Aurelia
Some AWS Lambda functions written in Typescript
Dozens of different testing, code quality and static analysis tools
Infrastructure is managed with Terraform on top of AWS
You don't need experience in all of these technologies to succeed - we look for curious folks with growth mindsets and an interest to work with this stack.

Key aspects of this role
Ship quality features end-to-end > own work from shaping through to deployment and beyond, with tests written as part of the process, not bolted on after
Be an active voice in design phase > contribute during the upfront design phase alongside developers, designers, the product lead, and users; your technical perspective should influence what gets built and how
Use AI as a force multiplier > leverage AI tooling throughout your entire workflow, from investigation to implementation to review; the expectation is fluency, not occasional use
Maintain and raise the quality bar > contribute to our testing culture, engage with static analysis and type safety tooling, and help us keep the codebase in a state we're proud of
Help grow the team around you > work closely with junior developers, share context generously, and contribute to a culture where the whole team gets better
Think critically, not just technically > push back on unclear requirements, ask why before writing code, and bring your judgment to product and architectural decisions, not just implementation

You’re ideal for the role if you have…
2+ years as a software engineer > ideally in a SaaS product environment where you've owned features end-to-end
AI Native Fluency > you use AI tools throughout your entire workflow, not just when you're stuck; you can talk concretely about how you use them & where you apply judgment
Modern frameworks > Experience/familiarity with modern Javascript/TypeScript frameworks (Vue / React / Angular / Aurelia) and/or modern PHP (Symfony / Laravel / CodeIgniter) - we expect some up-skilling/cross-skilling
Quality-obsession > you're excited by test-driven development, static analysis, and building things properly; quality isn't a chore to you, it's how you work
Enthusiastic problem solver > excited by solving technical challenges and building a product with a heavy focus on quality (test-driven development, infrastructure as code etc)
Critical thinking skills > you challenge vague specs, understand the problem before reaching for a solution, and bring genuine reasoning to decisions
Growth-oriented and mission-driven > you're hungry to improve, open to feedback, and want to grow with a scaling business; you thrive in a fast paced, ambiguous environment

Our culture
Remote First - work from anywhere in Australia - we were remote-first before Covid, have established processes around collaboration/culture to ensure inclusiveness & engagement.
Socials - We have regular online social events, quarterly in person socials & hold annual off-sites in person.
Team First - we’re close knit, highly collaborative, actively help each other succeed (knowledge sharing, training) to develop our teams’ capabilities
High Expectations - we’re self motivated, embrace velocity as our greatest advantage, take pride in what we do and push to reach our potential.
Radical Transparency - we believe in flat structures & sharing knowledge, everyone is privy to what happens day-to-day and nothing is hidden (i.e. company strategy, finances & all).
Extra Leave Days - A day off for your Birthday and an extra day off before Christmas

Compensation
$100k-120k + Super (salary based on experience)
Computer equipment allowance to ensure you have everything you need to be successful working remotely

How to apply
Hit Apply and fill out the form with your information, CV and answer to the question "Why do you want to work for ProcurePro?”

What happens next

You’ll receive a short assessment to complete [~ 2 hours]
Assessment presentation and discussion call [~1.5 hour]
If successful, we will check references & make you an offer!

Extras:
All our interviews are recorded and shared internally with the CEO and hiring manager. We do this so that our team can make better decisions.
Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so speed is key. The sooner you apply, the better!
If you love the sound of working for us and the role described but the title/salary doesn’t align to your experience, please don’t let that stop you applying. We celebrate diversity and we want people of all genders, races, ages, backgrounds, beliefs, and life experiences to join our team.
We complete police checks or the equivalent once you join as part of our SOC2 compliance requirements.
We cannot sponsor visas. You must be based in the country of the advertised role and have the right to work independently. Otherwise, we cannot proceed with your application. Yes, we are remote, but we can only employ in the country advertised.

About the job
Who We Are:

Spawnpoint Media is at the forefront of digital entertainment, spearheading innovation in content creation across YouTube channels and media brands within the realms of gaming, Minecraft, and Live Action content. With a passionate community of over 23 million followers, we've amassed more than 6 billion views and over 57 thousand years of content watched globally. We strive to maintain the soul, love, joy, and personality of youth in our content and are committed to our mission of leaving a lasting impact on families across the globe.

Who You Are

Our team is seeking a skilled and creative Java Game Developer, who can work with our incredible team of Minecraft Set Designers and Java Developers to bring YouTube scripts and storyboards to life in an interactive and exciting way through custom Minecraft Mods.

This is a casual position, working remotely.

Your Responsibilities And Skills

Minecraft Mod Programming: Develop and compile Minecraft: Java Edition mods based on provided YouTube scripts, encompassing various elements such as blocks, items, mobs, entities, UI, commands, and custom gameplay features.
Custom Functionality Implementation: Design blocks, items, mobs, and entities with unique functionalities and AI behaviour, enhancing gameplay dynamics within Minecraft.
UI and Command Customization: Create custom Minecraft UI elements and commands to enrich the player experience and introduce new gameplay mechanics.
Crafting Methods and Recipes: Implement custom crafting methods and recipes beyond the default Crafting Table, introducing novel crafting mechanics and item interactions.
Engaging Gameplay Design: Craft interesting and unique gameplay moments and level designs within the scripts, ensuring audience engagement and interest throughout the Minecraft experience.
Video Storyboard Scripts: Identifying mod requirements and tasks from video storyboard scripts
Collaborative Set Design Support: Collaborate with the Set Design team to develop mods that complement and enhance the physical set designs, facilitating efficient and practical script implementation.
Integration with Modellers: Work closely with Modellers to integrate code and gameplay mechanics into tangible Minecraft models, bringing virtual elements to life within the game environment.
Audience-Centric Mod Development: Develop mods that resonate with the core audience of the videos, primarily young children, by ensuring visual appeal and engaging gameplay functionalities.

Requirements:

Minimal requirement - v1.20+ modding experience
Proficient in Java programming language
Developing Minecraft mods for NeoForge in Java, typically on Minecraft 1.20.1 utilising existing/pre-provided assets
Must be familiar with Eclipse and/or Intellij Java IDEs.
Full understanding of Minecraft and all game mechanics
Translate design ideas into functional game code
Troubleshooting and rectifying issues with developed mods
Understands how to navigate and read through technical documentation online in order to advance one's understanding of the subject matter and/or resolve bugs.
Ability to interpret instructions and create modded content from pre-existing visual assets in a relatively quick turnaround
Ability to investigate and resolve basic modding issues both in your own code and others
Ability to read and interpret Minecraft crash reports and log files to identify issues
Familiarity with GeckoLib and its usage as a dependency in modding
Ability to understand feedback and act on it.

Preferable Additional Skills

Understands what a JSON file is and how to create or edit one (loot tables, tags, etc)
Experience working in Java Edition/Datapacks/1.17+ Command Syntax/NBT data
Understands basic Minecraft related development terms, such as entity, datapack, addon, behaviours, components, function, animation controllers, etc
Understands basic programming logic/design (scoreboard loops, execute if’s, operators, etc).
Familiar with Blockbench (or Cubik Studio/some other modelling program)
Familiar with Aseprite, Paint.net, Photoshop, or some other form of editing software.
Ability to make small modifications or changes to existing models/assets in the event it is needed

Spawnpoint Perks:

Attractive Remuneration
Spawn Points KPI bonus Program
Training & Development
Career Progression

Our Offer

If you’re excited to develop some of the most-loved content on the internet and bring smiles to millions of young people across the globe; whilst working alongside a highly skilled and fun-loving team; passionate about all things YouTube and gaming, then what are you waiting for? We really love working here, and we think you will too! Apply now!
